zh.lifeSummary: 當Constance de Bloigho於1245年出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾聖 奧斯特爾時，她的父親Alan de Bloigho20歲，母親Maud Matilda Hewis20歲。她於大約1267年與Sir Oger Keningk Lord of Trigg在英國英格蘭可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Constance de Bloigho was born in 1245, in St Austell, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, her father, Alan de Bloigho, was 20 and her mother, Maud Matilda Hewis, was 20. She married Sir Oger Keningk Lord of Trigg about 1267, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Constance de Bloigho est née en 1245, à St Austell, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alan de Bloigho, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Maud Matilda Hewis, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Oger Keningk Lord of Trigg environ 1267, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

Lei sposato Sir Oger Keningk Lord of Trigg circa 1267, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Constance de Bloighoが1245年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルスト アウステルで生まれた時，父Alan de Bloighoは20歳，母Maud Matilda Hewisは20歳であった。約1267年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルでSir Oger Keningk Lord of Triggと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Constance de Bloigho(이)가 1245년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 오스텔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alan de Bloigho(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Matilda Hewis(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1267년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 Sir Oger Keningk Lord of Trigg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Constance de Bloigho родилась в 1245 г. в местности ст аустелл, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Alan de Bloigho было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Matilda Hewis, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Oger Keningk Lord of Trigg около 1267 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в её родном городе.
